Collections¶

Quaternion collection types — QuatVector, QuatMatrix, QuatTensor.

class quat.collections.QuatVector(data)[source]¶

Bases: object

Parameters:

data (np.ndarray | list | tuple | QuatVector)

classmethod zeros(n)[source]¶
Parameters:

n (int)

Return type:

QuatVector

classmethod ones(n)[source]¶
Parameters:

n (int)

Return type:

QuatVector

property shape: Tuple[int, ...]¶
property data: ndarray¶
to_array()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

to_numpy(copy=True, dtype=None)[source]¶
Parameters:

copy (bool)

Return type:

ndarray

property real: ndarray¶
property i: ndarray¶
property j: ndarray¶
property k: ndarray¶
quaternions()[source]¶
Return type:

List[Quaternion]

inner(other)[source]¶

Quaternion-valued inner product.

Example

>>> a = QuatVector.zeros(3)
>>> b = QuatVector.zeros(3)
>>> ip = a.inner(b)
Parameters:

other (QuatVector)

Return type:

Quaternion

norm()[source]¶

Frobenius norm of the vector.

Example

>>> v = QuatVector.ones(3)
>>> v.norm()
1.732...
Return type:

float

norm_squared()[source]¶
Return type:

float

normalize()[source]¶

Return a unit-norm copy of this vector.

Example

>>> v = QuatVector.ones(3)
>>> u = v.normalize()
>>> u.norm()
1.0
Return type:

QuatVector

isnan()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

isinf()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

isfinite()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

Parameters:

  • other (QuatVector)

  • rtol (float)

  • atol (float)

Return type:

ndarray

Return type:

str

Return type:

bytes

Parameters:

s (str)

Return type:

QuatVector

classmethod from_bytes(b)[source]¶
Parameters:

b (bytes)

Return type:

QuatVector

to_complex_matrix()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

to_real_matrix_left()[source]¶

Return left-multiplication real matrix of shape (4n, 4).

Example

>>> v = QuatVector.ones(2)
>>> M = v.to_real_matrix_left()
>>> M.shape
(8, 4)
Return type:

ndarray

classmethod from_real_matrix_left(M)[source]¶
Parameters:

M (ndarray)

Return type:

QuatVector

QuatVector

ndarray

copy (bool)

ndarray

property real: ndarray¶
property i: ndarray¶
property j: ndarray¶
property k: ndarray¶
transpose()[source]¶

Quaternion matrix transpose.

Example

>>> A = QuatMatrix.zeros(2, 3)
>>> A.T.shape
(3, 2)
Return type:

QuatMatrix

property T: QuatMatrix¶

Alias for transpose().

conjugate()[source]¶

Entrywise quaternion conjugate.

Return type:

QuatMatrix

adjoint()[source]¶

Quaternion conjugate transpose (adjoint).

Example

>>> A = QuatMatrix.zeros(2, 3)
>>> A.H.shape
(3, 2)
Return type:

QuatMatrix

norm()[source]¶
Return type:

float

norm_squared()[source]¶
Return type:

float

property H: QuatMatrix¶

Alias for adjoint().

Return type:

ndarray

ndarray

ndarray

ndarray

Parameters:

M (ndarray)

Return type:

QuatMatrix

to_real_matrix_left()[source]¶

Return left-multiplication real matrix of shape (4m, 4n).

Example

>>> A = QuatMatrix.eye(2)
>>> M = A.to_real_matrix_left()
>>> M.shape
(8, 8)
Return type:

ndarray

classmethod from_real_matrix_left(M)[source]¶
Parameters:

M (ndarray)

Return type:

QuatMatrix

Parameters:

data (np.ndarray | list | tuple | QuatTensor)

Parameters:

  • p (int)

  • q (int)

  • r (int)

Return type:

QuatTensor

property shape: Tuple[int, int, int]¶
property ndim: int¶
property data: ndarray¶
to_array()[source]¶
Return type:

ndarray

Parameters:

copy (bool)

Return type:

ndarray

property real: ndarray¶
property i: ndarray¶
property j: ndarray¶
property k: ndarray¶
mode_1_product(A)[source]¶

Contract first mode with quaternion matrix A.

Example

>>> X = QuatTensor.zeros(3, 4, 5)
>>> A = QuatMatrix.zeros(2, 3)
>>> Y = X.mode_1_product(A)
>>> Y.shape
(2, 4, 5)
Parameters:

A (QuatMatrix)

Return type:

QuatTensor

mode_2_product(A)[source]¶
Parameters:

A (QuatMatrix)

Return type:

QuatTensor

mode_3_product(A)[source]¶
Parameters:

A (QuatMatrix)

Return type:

QuatTensor

Parameters:

other (QuatTensor)

Return type:

Quaternion

Return type:

float

Return type:

float

Return type:

ndarray

Return type:

ndarray

Return type:

ndarray

  • atol (float)

ndarray

str

Return type:

bytes

classmethod from_json(s)[source]¶
Parameters:

s (str)

Return type:

QuatTensor

classmethod from_bytes(b)[source]¶
Parameters:

b (bytes)

Return type:

QuatTensor

unfold(mode)[source]¶

Unfold tensor along mode into a QuatMatrix.

Example

>>> X = QuatTensor.zeros(3, 4, 5)
>>> X.unfold(1).shape
(3, 20)
>>> X.unfold(2).shape
(4, 15)
>>> X.unfold(3).shape
(5, 12)
Parameters:

mode (int)

Return type:

QuatMatrix

to_complex_matrix(mode=1)[source]¶
Parameters:

mode (int)

Return type:

ndarray

to_real_matrix_left(mode=1)[source]¶
Parameters:

mode (int)

Return type:

ndarray

quat.collections.dict_to_quat_matrix(X_dict)[source]¶

Convert a single-sample quaternion dict to QuatMatrix.

X_dict: {‘real’: (H,W), ‘i’: (H,W), ‘j’: (H,W), ‘k’: (H,W)} Returns: QuatMatrix of shape (H, W) — one Quaternion per pixel.

Example

>>> import numpy as np
>>> X = {'real': np.zeros((2,3)), 'i': np.zeros((2,3)),
...      'j': np.zeros((2,3)), 'k': np.zeros((2,3))}
>>> M = dict_to_quat_matrix(X)
>>> M.shape
(2, 3)
Parameters:

X_dict (dict)

Return type:

QuatMatrix

quat.collections.dict_to_quat_tensor(X_dict)[source]¶

Convert a batched quaternion dict to QuatTensor.

Equivalent to stacking per-sample dict_to_quat_matrix results.

X_dict: {‘real’: (n,H,W), ‘i’: (n,H,W), ‘j’: (n,H,W), ‘k’: (n,H,W)} Returns: QuatTensor of shape (n, H, W) — mode1=sample, mode2=H, mode3=W

Parameters:

X_dict (dict)

Return type:

QuatTensor

quat.collections.labels_to_quat_vector(y, binary=False)[source]¶

Convert integer labels to a pure-real QuatVector.

y: (n,) int array binary: if True, maps 0→-1, 1→+1

Example

>>> import numpy as np
>>> y = np.array([0, 1, 2])
>>> v = labels_to_quat_vector(y)
>>> v.shape
(3,)
>>> labels_to_quat_vector(y, binary=True).real
array([-1.,  1.,  2.])
Parameters:

  • y (ndarray)

  • binary (bool)

Return type:

QuatVector
